claim: Prompts are suitable for one-off tasks but create wasted effort when used repeatedly; they do not package workflows in a reusable way
actionable: Use prompts only for temporary, single-use tasks; move to skills or plugins for anything you repeat regularly
source_channel: AI News & Strategy Daily | Nate B Jones
source_title: You're Wasting 40% Of Your AI Time On Something Fixable

claim: Skills are markdown documents that encode a team's house style and should be used for repeatable workflows, independent of the LLM being used
actionable: Write skills as clear markdown documents describing your process, then apply the same skill across different LLMs (Claude, Codex) without modification
source_channel: AI News & Strategy Daily | Nate B Jones
source_title: You're Wasting 40% Of Your AI Time On Something Fixable

claim: Plugins are larger packages than skills that include workflows, app integrations, MCP servers, hooks, scripts, and assets—making them shareable and installable by entire teams
actionable: Use plugins to wrap complete workflows that require data connectors, external tools, or complex infrastructure; skills alone are not sufficient
source_channel: AI News & Strategy Daily | Nate B Jones
source_title: You're Wasting 40% Of Your AI Time On Something Fixable

claim: MCPs (Model Context Protocol) and app connectors provide live access to external systems where work happens, allowing agents to pull real data rather than simulate it
actionable: Use MCPs to connect agents to live data sources (Salesforce, GitHub, Slack) so agents work with real information instead of hallucinating
source_channel: AI News & Strategy Daily | Nate B Jones
source_title: You're Wasting 40% Of Your AI Time On Something Fixable

claim: Hooks and scripts should handle deterministic operations (validation, formatting, running tests) rather than relying on model judgment; models should not be asked to verify their own output
actionable: Build scripts to verify JSON validity, run tests, and validate schemas deterministically; do not ask the model to imagine running tests or checking its own work
source_channel: AI News & Strategy Daily | Nate B Jones
source_title: You're Wasting 40% Of Your AI Time On Something Fixable

claim: The 20/80 rule applies to skills: 20% of skills will deliver 80% of value; focus on identifying and building the most-repeated, highest-impact skills
actionable: Prioritize building skills for workflows that repeat frequently, have high sensitivity to quality, and align with your team's core processes
source_channel: AI News & Strategy Daily | Nate B Jones
source_title: You're Wasting 40% Of Your AI Time On Something Fixable

claim: RLHF and RLVR are both policy gradient methods; the real innovation is not the algorithm but the input data quality and signal trustworthiness
actionable: When evaluating post-training methods, focus on the quality of training signals (human preference vs. verifiable math) rather than algorithmic variants
source_channel: AI News & Strategy Daily | Nate B Jones
source_title: You're Wasting 40% Of Your AI Time On Something Fixable

claim: Understanding the scaffolding around an LLM (prompts, skills, plugins, MCPs, hooks, scripts) is more important than the base model quality for practical AI work
actionable: Invest time in designing your agentic scaffolding; proper architecture multiplies the effectiveness of any LLM by 10x
source_channel: AI News & Strategy Daily | Nate B Jones
source_title: You're Wasting 40% Of Your AI Time On Something Fixable
